quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Incomplete dominance"?

options.text: Direct transfer of a phosphate group from a substrate molecule to ADP to form ATP; occurs in glycolysis and the Krebs cycle
The molecular machine for translation; composed of large and small subunits containing rRNA and proteins; has A, P, and E sites
Failure of chromosomes to separate properly during meiosis, resulting in gametes with abnormal chromosome numbers (aneuploidy)
Heterozygotes show an intermediate phenotype between two homozygotes (e.g., Cʀ Cᵂ = pink flowers)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Incomplete dominance" is defined as: Heterozygotes show an intermediate phenotype between two homozygotes (e.g., Cʀ Cᵂ = pink flowers). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Codominance"?

options.text: Both alleles are fully expressed in the heterozygote (e.g., Iᴬ Iᴮ blood type produces both A and B antigens)
Non-native organisms that spread aggressively in new environments, outcompeting native species (e.g., kudzu, cane toads, zebra mussels)
v = Vmax[S]/(Km + [S]); describes the relationship between substrate concentration and reaction rate for many enzymes
No mutation, no migration, random mating, no selection, large population size; violations drive evolution

quiz_questions.explanation: "Codominance" is defined as: Both alleles are fully expressed in the heterozygote (e.g., Iᴬ Iᴮ blood type produces both A and B antigens). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Polygenic inheritance"?

options.text: The 4-carbon molecule that accepts acetyl-CoA to begin each turn of the Krebs cycle; regenerated at the end of each turn
Multiple genes contribute to a single trait, producing continuous variation (e.g., skin color, height, intelligence)
Membrane-bound organelle containing chromatin (DNA + histones); site of DNA replication, transcription, and ribosome assembly (nucleolus)
A 3-carbon sugar produced by the Calvin cycle; the direct product used to build glucose, amino acids, and fatty acids

quiz_questions.explanation: "Polygenic inheritance" is defined as: Multiple genes contribute to a single trait, producing continuous variation (e.g., skin color, height, intelligence). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Epistasis"?

options.text: RuBisCO fixes O₂ instead of CO₂, producing a 2-carbon compound that wastes energy; more common at high temperatures
A phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins that controls what enters and exits the cell; selectively permeable
One gene affects the expression of another gene at a different locus (e.g., coat color in Labrador retrievers: B/b for color, E/e for deposition)
Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes during prophase I of meiosis; increases genetic variation

quiz_questions.explanation: "Epistasis" is defined as: One gene affects the expression of another gene at a different locus (e.g., coat color in Labrador retrievers: B/b for color, E/e for deposition). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Sex-linked (X-linked) inheritance"?

options.text: A species whose impact on community structure is disproportionately large relative to its abundance (e.g., sea otters, wolves in Yellowstone)
Genes on the X chromosome; males (XY) express all X-linked alleles; conditions like hemophilia and color blindness are more common in males
A technique separating DNA, RNA, or protein fragments by size using an electric field; smaller fragments migrate faster through the agarose or polyacrylamide gel
The enzyme that synthesizes ATP as H⁺ flows down its concentration gradient from the intermembrane space back to the matrix

quiz_questions.explanation: "Sex-linked (X-linked) inheritance" is defined as: Genes on the X chromosome; males (XY) express all X-linked alleles; conditions like hemophilia and color blindness are more common in males. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Heterozygotes show an intermediate phenotype between two homozygotes (e.g., Cʀ Cᵂ = pink flowers)"?

options.text: Incomplete dominance
Metastasis
Glycolysis
Missense mutation

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Incomplete dominance".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Both alleles are fully expressed in the heterozygote (e.g., Iᴬ Iᴮ blood type produces both A and B antigens)"?

options.text: Habitat destruction
Crossing over
Codominance
Cellular respiration (overall)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Codominance".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Multiple genes contribute to a single trait, producing continuous variation (e.g., skin color, height, intelligence)"?

options.text: Cohesion
Proton-motive force
Photorespiration
Polygenic inheritance

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Polygenic inheritance".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "One gene affects the expression of another gene at a different locus (e.g., coat color in Labrador retrievers: B/b for color, E/e for deposition)"?

options.text: Mutagen
Species richness
Homologous structures
Epistasis

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Epistasis".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Genes on the X chromosome; males (XY) express all X-linked alleles; conditions like hemophilia and color blindness are more common in males"?

options.text: Hydrogen bond
Law of Segregation
Sex-linked (X-linked) inheritance
Independent assortment

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Sex-linked (X-linked) inheritance".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Non-Mendelian Inheritance is accurate?

options.text: Parsimony (fewest evolutionary changes) and maximum likelihood methods are used to determine the most probable tree topology.

Non-Mendelian Inheritance includes patterns that do not follow simple dominant-recessive rules: incomplete dominance (blending phenotypes, e.g., red × white = pink in snapdragons), codominance (both alleles fully expressed, e.g., AB blood type),...
These properties arise from water's bent molecular geometry and electronegativity difference between O and H.
When damage is too severe, p53 activates apoptosis to prevent propagation of mutations that could lead to cancer.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Non-Mendelian Inheritance as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Non-Mendelian Inheritance?

options.text: Logistic growth (dN/dt = rN(K-N)/K) incorporates carrying capacity K and produces S-shaped curves.
Genomic technologies (DNA sequencing, gene editing with CRISPR-Cas9, and bioinformatics) have revolutionized biology, medicine, agriculture, and forensics.
Properties of Water make it essential for life due to its polar covalent bonds and hydrogen bonding.
Sex-linked traits show characteristic patterns because males are hemizygous for X-linked genes.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Non-Mendelian Inheritance as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Non-Mendelian Inheritance?

options.text: Photosystems II and I absorb photons, exciting electrons that pass through electron transport chains.

Non-Mendelian Inheritance includes patterns that do not follow simple dominant-recessive rules: incomplete dominance (blending phenotypes, e.g., red × white = pink in snapdragons), codominance (both alleles fully expressed, e.g., AB blood type),...
Cell Division (Mitosis and Meiosis) are the two types of nuclear division.
Phospholipids form the basis of cell membrane bilayers due to their amphipathic nature (hydrophilic head, hydrophobic tails).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Non-Mendelian Inheritance as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Non-Mendelian Inheritance?

options.text: Mutation and Cancer explains how accumulated mutations in proto-oncogenes (gain-of-function to oncogenes) and tumor suppressor genes (loss-of-function) drive the multistep development of cancer.

The Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium (p² + 2pq + q² = 1, p + q = 1) serves as a null hypothesis: populations are in equilibrium only when there is no mutation, migration, selection, drift, or non-random mating.
Logistic growth (dN/dt = rN(K-N)/K) incorporates carrying capacity K and produces S-shaped curves.
Sex-linked traits show characteristic patterns because males are hemizygous for X-linked genes.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Non-Mendelian Inheritance as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Non-Mendelian Inheritance?

options.text: The Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium (p² + 2pq + q² = 1, p + q = 1) serves as a null hypothesis: populations are in equilibrium only when there is no mutation, migration, selection, drift, or non-random mating.

Non-Mendelian Inheritance includes patterns that do not follow simple dominant-recessive rules: incomplete dominance (blending phenotypes, e.g., red × white = pink in snapdragons), codominance (both alleles fully expressed, e.g., AB blood type),...
Water is split (photolysis) at PSII, releasing O₂.
Population viability analysis (PVA) estimates extinction probability for small populations considering demographic and environmental stochasticity.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Non-Mendelian Inheritance as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Non-Mendelian Inheritance?

options.text: Amazon deforestation averaging ~10,000 km² per year, threatening the most biodiverse terrestrial ecosystem with an estimated 10% of all species on Earth
Madagascar's unique biodiversity (90% of species found nowhere else) explained by 88 million years of isolation after separating from Africa and India
Exercise physiology: transitioning from aerobic to anaerobic respiration during intense activity when O₂ delivery cannot meet demand
ABO blood type compatibility in transfusions determined by codominant Iᴬ and Iᴮ alleles and recessive i allele (3 alleles, 6 genotypes, 4 phenotypes)

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Non-Mendelian Inheritance.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Non-Mendelian Inheritance?

options.text: Lac operon as a model for understanding antibiotic resistance gene regulation in bacteria, where environmental signals trigger expression of resistance genes
Human skin color as a polygenic trait involving at least 15 genes, producing continuous variation across populations adapted to different UV radiation levels
Cancer as uncontrolled mitosis: mutations in proto-oncogenes and tumor suppressors enable cells to bypass cell cycle checkpoints

Epinephrine (adrenaline) signaling cascade: one epinephrine molecule activates many G-proteins, each activating adenylyl cyclase producing millions of cAMP molecules, amplifying the fight-or-flight response

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Non-Mendelian Inheritance.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Non-Mendelian Inheritance applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Hemophilia in European royal families: X-linked recessive inheritance traced through pedigrees from Queen Victoria to multiple royal houses
Antioxidant research examining whether dietary antioxidants (vitamins C, E) can reduce oxidative DNA damage from reactive oxygen species
Yellowstone-to-Yukon Conservation Initiative creating wildlife corridors spanning 3,200 km to connect fragmented habitats for grizzly bears, wolves, and elk migration
Insulin signaling pathway: insulin binding to its receptor triggers a kinase cascade leading to glucose transporter (GLUT4) insertion into cell membranes

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Non-Mendelian Inheritance.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Non-Mendelian Inheritance?

options.text: Cell membrane fluidity regulation through cholesterol insertion between phospholipids and adjustment of saturated vs. unsaturated fatty acid ratios
Detecting natural selection by comparing observed genotype frequencies to Hardy-Weinberg predictions and identifying significant deviations using chi-square tests
Metabolic interconnections: Krebs cycle intermediates serve as precursors for amino acid synthesis, fatty acid synthesis, and gluconeogenesis
ABO blood type compatibility in transfusions determined by codominant Iᴬ and Iᴮ alleles and recessive i allele (3 alleles, 6 genotypes, 4 phenotypes)

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Non-Mendelian Inheritance.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Non-Mendelian Inheritance in practice?

options.text: RT-PCR diagnostic tests detecting viral RNA (COVID-19) by reverse-transcribing RNA to DNA and amplifying specific sequences
Artificial photosynthesis research mimicking PSII's water-splitting capability to generate hydrogen fuel using sunlight
Human skin color as a polygenic trait involving at least 15 genes, producing continuous variation across populations adapted to different UV radiation levels
Targeted cancer therapy: Herceptin (trastuzumab) blocking the HER2 receptor in breast cancers that overexpress this oncogene protein

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Non-Mendelian Inheritance.
